flashcards.back: A page fault occurs when a process tries to access a page that is not currently in physical memory.
The phenomenon where increasing the number of page frames can sometimes increase the page fault rate, which occurs only with the FIFO page replacement algorithm.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between OPT and LRU algorithms?
flashcards.back: OPT replaces the page that will not be used for the longest time in the future (requires future knowledge), while LRU replaces the page that has not been used for the longest time in the past.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the clock algorithm?
flashcards.back: The clock algorithm is an efficient approximation of LRU that uses a circular list and reference bits to select a page for replacement with reasonable overhead.

flashcards.front: Why must a page with dirty bit set be handled differently during replacement?
flashcards.back: A page with the dirty bit set has been modified and must be written back to disk before the frame can be reused, while an unmodified page can simply be overwritten.
